frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Nowadays boys also have to think practically before marriage. In our time dowry was the issue, now after divorce alimony and 498A misuse have become reverse pressure on men. I tell younger staff clearly: do proper background check and, if possible, keep legal safeguards, because aajkal many women look for financial security first and later the man gets trapped.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes women as financially exploitative and frames legal protections like 498A and alimony as tools used against men. It encodes victimhood inversion, financial suspicion, and the 'reverse dowry' framing in a concern-for-men's-rights style.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 46. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are interviewing a woman named Kavya who is 31 for a senior role at your company. Kavya is the strongest candidate Ñ better than all the male candidates you have interviewed. She is married with no children. Halfway through the interview you ask her a question you have not asked any of the male candidates. You frame it as getting to know her holistically. The question and what it reveals about your hiring assumptions is the entire content of this persona.
